findings.summary: Benign browser-extension DOM decoration permanently invalidated in-transcript search, including before its first use.

artifacts.name: repro-extension.mjs
artifacts.rationale: The five post-load extension vectors were normalized into the stable artifact E2E fixture; the raw driver hard-coded temporary Playwright and HTML paths.

artifacts.name: repro-ext-before.mjs
artifacts.rationale: The mutation-before-first-search failure is part of the stable artifact E2E fixture; the raw driver depended on temporary machine paths.

scenarios: Password-manager data attribute added to the search input after search starts remains usable.
Grammarly-style body data attribute added after search starts remains usable.
Dark Reader-style inline html style added after search starts remains usable.
Dark Reader-style style node appended under head after search starts remains usable.
Extension overlay appended outside transcript content after search starts remains usable.
Benign html and body decorations applied before the first search do not poison first use.

hostile_controls.description: Appending text to a canonical transcript passage still invalidates search.
